programmes.show_description: James Martin takes a road trip to the country that inspired his cooking career. Travelling in Keith Floyd's motor, he creates delicious new recipes in idyllic locations. S1, Ep20
Seafood, Spice And Soul. Guy Fieri checks out seaside snacks, global spice, and Southern soul. In Georgia, he visits a Tybee Island joint serving gourmet hot dogs! S37, Ep6
Soup, Sandwiches And Seafood. Guy Fieri dives into amped-up eats, from Nashville hot chicken at a coastal Georgia joint and savoury Vietnamese street food in a Calgary noodle bar. S38, Ep14
Old Time Attitude. In Nashville, Guy checks out an award-winning restaurant where they have mastered a southern classic. Plus, he heads to a barbecue eatery in Georgia. S9, Ep13
Italian. Guy finds great Italian food in places people would never expect, like a Tennessee drive-in where they make their own meat sauce and bring the pasta to the customer's car. S1, Ep2
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Richmond, Virginia. Casey Webb visits Richmond for a lesson on New Zealand-style savoury pies. Plus, he enjoys an elevated Jewish deli experience and a mighty burger challenge. S5, Ep3
Virginia Beach, Virginia. Casey Webb visits Virginia Beach. He enjoys a street corn fritter-topped burger, a decadent croissant-muffin hybrid and a corned beef omelette challenge. S5, Ep4
Corsica. The bikers begin in the south of Corsica. They taste sausage figatelli, veal and olive stew, and stuffed mussels with courgette gratin. S1, Ep3
Quarterback Mom. Ree's quarterback teens have a big game coming up so she makes their favourite food, including meatball subs, spicy mac and cheese, and confetti blondies. S24, Ep10
Birthday for Two. It's Ree and Ladd's birthdays so they celebrate with skillet spaghetti with meatballs and garlic bread. Then, they enjoy a black forest cake for dessert. S24, Ep11
Jamie Oliver whips up speedy smashed lemongrass chicken, flavour-packed sweet potato chilli with homemade nachos, and a chocolate party tray cake. S1, Ep5
Jamie Oliver cooks his take on shakshuka with asparagus, broccoli and harissa. Plus, he shares a recipe for pulled pork that can be batched up. S1, Ep6
Cooking With Friends. It's competition time as Ina and her friends, Eli Zabar and David Ziff, cook a Mediterranean-inspired meal. Plus, tasty three-berries pancakes for breakfast. S6, Ep3
Rehearsal Dinner. Ina prepares a fool proof menu for any special occasion. The buffet table includes tarragon potato salad, chunky blue cheese and vegetable coleslaw. S6, Ep4
Patisserie. Amateur bakers attempt to make petits fours in a bid to reach the final, under the watchful eyes of judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood. But who will triumph? S3, Ep9
The Final. After weeks of pastries, cakes and bread, three bakers have made it. They now face the most demanding of challenges as every aspect of their baking skill is scrutinised. S3, Ep10
James's adventure takes him to St Émilion, where he first fell in love with food. He visits the Chateau Cheval Blanc and the L'Hostellerie Plaisance kitchens. S1, Ep1
James travels to the glorious Canal du Midi where he indulges in delicious seafood. He trades a little work aboard a boat for a traditional cuttlefish treat. S1, Ep2
Corsica. The bikers begin in the south of Corsica. They taste sausage figatelli, veal and olive stew, and stuffed mussels with courgette gratin. S1, Ep3
Quarterback Mom. Ree's quarterback teens have a big game coming up so she makes their favourite food, including meatball subs, spicy mac and cheese, and confetti blondies. S24, Ep10

The Final. After weeks of pastries, cakes and bread, three bakers have made it. They now face the most demanding of challenges as every aspect of their baking skill is scrutinised. S3, Ep10
Sunny Lunches. Marcus looks for lunch ingredients in the market in Palma. He makes chilled tomato soup, then chop chop salad with watermelon, pickled rind, and feta. S1, Ep1
Cooking Over Fire. Marcus visits a chef who cooks everything on his outside BBQ using wood grown on his farm. Inspired, Marcus fires up his BBQ to make pork chops. S1, Ep2
Rick Stein goes on a pigeon shoot in Cornwall, inspiring him to cook pigeon and peas. Plus, after visiting Devon, he makes a warm skate salad. S1, Ep1
Rick Stein cooks sautéed chicken with 40 cloves of garlic and a warm pheasant salad. Plus, he learns how cider is made at an old-fashioned cider farm in Somerset. S1, Ep2
Lights-Out Latin. Guy checks out amazing Latin specialities from Peruvian sandwiches in Miami T Panama flavours in North Carolina to a joint in Arizona serving tacos and carne asada. S28, Ep7
East Coast, West Coast. Guy hits both coasts for tasty dishes. In California, he grabs spicy pork and plantains and visits a bakery-restaurant serving up meatloaf in North Carolina. S28, Ep12
Louisville, KY. Casey Webb is in Louisville, Kentucky, for epic eats including a booze-infused Southern staple and a spicy popcorn and frozen hot chocolate challenge in Indiana. S2, Ep3
Charleston. Adam Richman heads to Charleston, South Carolina for oysters served by the shovel, a fried chicken sandwich smothered in sausage gravy, and a spicy tuna sushi challenge. S2, Ep4
All Kinds Of Barbecue. Guy takes a southern swing to check out different styles of barbecue, including sweet ribs in Mississippi and tangy pulled pork in New Orleans. S3, Ep12
Diners-A-Plenty. Guy Fieri tries a diner in South Carolina where the specialty is the A-Plenty, a meal or sandwich covered in French fries and onion rings. S1, Ep11
Diggerland, Dol And Dresses. Mauro makes a Korean-themed first birthday cake, Madeline picks a dress for her wedding anniversary. And, Buddy creates a cake for a digger theme park! S9, Ep5
Go Fly A Kite. Buddy judges a true 'clash-of-the-titans' - a cook-off between local police and fire-fighters, complete with two fabulous cakes to celebrate the event! S9, Ep6

Mainland France. The bikers begin in Marseille, one of the largest ports in the Mediterranean. There, they sample bourride, a delicious fish stew. S1, Ep4
Ask Me Anything Wildcard. Ree Drummond answers questions from friends, townsfolk and visitors to Pawhuska. She shares her ribeye banh mi Vietnamese sandwich plus other tasty recipes. S24, Ep12
Fire And Feast. Ree Drummond cooks a spring feast while Ladd and the cowboys are busy on the ranch. She makes pork tenderloin with herby mustard cream and mac and cheese primavera. S24, Ep13
Jamie Oliver cooks a super-speedy smoked salmon pasta, a loaded beef burger with sweet red peppers, pickles and mustard, and toffee apple buns. S1, Ep7
Jamie cooks an aubergine burger, a sesame-seared salmon supper, and a chickpea and squash casserole that can be turned into a host of tasty dishes. S1, Ep8
Dare To Be Different. Ina adapts classic dishes by adding new flavours and altering the cooking method. She makes an easy Parmesan risotto and deconstructed strawberry shortcakes. S6, Ep5
Modern Comfort. Ina makes classic comfort foods. Her friend, Mary Giuliani, helps Ina to make miniature grilled cheese sandwich canapés, French toast pudding and a seafood pot pie. S6, Ep6
Cake. The bakers face judges Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry and are asked to produce sandwich cakes, angel food cake and amazing chocolate creations. S4, Ep1
Bread. The surviving bakers face judges Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry and are asked to produce bread sticks, English muffins and a variety of loaves. S4, Ep2
James heads to the Languedoc, known as the heart of authentic France. He's treated to mouth-watering cassoulet and discovers delicious local ingredients. S1, Ep3
James enjoys the picturesque views and delicious food in Charente. He uses Cognac for his first al fresco cook and makes a classic French onion soup. S1, Ep4

programmes.show_name: New: Jamie Oliver: Cooking For Less
programmes.show_description: With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep1
The Ultimate Burger. Jamie Oliver takes the UK's favourite cuisine to the next level. The chef shows how to make his ultimate burger with his friend, DJ BBQ. S1, Ep1
Jamie Oliver masters Bolognese ravioli. Plus, he recreates the UK's favourite dish, chicken tikka masala - with a secret ingredient. S1, Ep2
Eastern Southern. Guy Fieri heads east to get a taste of the South. He checks out meatloaf in New Hampshire, extra-spicy shrimp in North Carolina and seafood stew in Florida. S36, Ep3
Beef And Bird. Guy Fieri loads up the meat! In New Hampshire, a funky joint puts Thai in their tacos, and a local gem in North Carolina fires up monster barbecue. S36, Ep5
